backlog.title: Restore markdown-import verbatim-on-error (Issue 1) & confirm test isolation (Issue 2)
backlog.description: A focused correctness bugfix changeset addressing the two QA findings in the Bug Fix Requirements PRD. Issue 1 (Major): a delivered markdown file that imports a file which EXISTS (stat OK, isFile OK) but is UNREADABLE (readFile EACCES/I-O) currently has its `#@`/`@` marker STRIPPED to a bare path inside the parent's emitted block, with no block appended — a silent violation of the PRD §5.4 / §5.6 step-4 / §12.5 / §12.16 'leave verbatim on read-error' guarantee and the README 'permission error → Left as written' table. The fix gates the markdown-import marker-strip on READABILITY (not just existence) so strip ⟺ deliverable. Issue 2 (Minor): the `readConfig` test case T2.S1-f isolation gap — REALITY-CHECK: already fixed in the working tree (uncommitted), 120/120 tests pass; this phase confirms and retains it. No Critical issues. NOTE: the image-resizeImage-THROWS variant of Issue 1 is a documented residual (resizeImage is contractually null-on-failure, not throw) and is out of scope for this changeset — see plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/bugfix/001_0aea8e8cbcc6/architecture/code_changes_analysis.md.

milestones.title: Issue 1: Restore verbatim-on-error for unreadable markdown imports
milestones.description: Restore the 'strip ⟺ deliverable' invariant in the markdown transitive-import path. Two coordinated, low-risk changes to file-injector.ts: (1) the PRIMARY fix — add an `fs.access(abs, R_OK)` readability gate to the `injectMarkdown` Step 3.5 injectability filter so a marker is stripped only when the import is genuinely readable (this alone fixes the reported bug); (2) the SECONDARY hygiene fix — un-claim `abs` from `injectedSet` on `injectFile`'s failure path so a failed read does not poison the dedup set ('claim ⟺ delivered'). Each subtask ships its own test per the implicit TDD workflow. The missing/directory/readable happy paths are unchanged.

tasks.title: Gate markdown-import marker stripping on readability + un-claim on failure
tasks.description: The bug lives in `injectMarkdown` (file-injector.ts:824-877). Its Step 3.5 (lines 848-856) filters scanTokens records to `injectable` using ONLY `fs.stat(r.abs)` + `st.isFile()` — it confirms the import is a regular file but NOT that it is readable. Step 4 (862-865) then strips the marker from every injectable record, Step 5 (867-868) emits the parent block (pre-order, BEFORE the read), and Step 6 (870-875) recurses into `injectFile` where `fs.readFile` (line 689) finally runs and can throw. So an exists-but-unreadable import gets stripped then never delivers. Contrast: the top-level path (processTokenStream:648-666) strips by post-hoc success (`if (ok) resolved.push(...)`) and is already correct. This task closes the asymmetry: S1 adds the R_OK gate; S2 cleans up the related claim-before-read poisoning.

subtasks.title: Add R_OK readability gate to injectMarkdown Step 3.5 + regression test
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/bugfix/001_0aea8e8cbcc6/architecture/system_context.md (§'Data flow through the bug site') and code_changes_analysis.md (§'Fix site 1'). The bug is CONFIRMED by a live repro: api.md (chmod 000, stat-OK/readFile-EACCES) imported by notes.md → marker `#@api.md` stripped to bare `api.md`, no `<file>` block, `r.text.includes('#@api.md')`===false (should be true). The existing top-level read-error case E4 (file-injector.test.mjs:600-615) PASSES because processTokenStream strips by post-hoc success; the markdown path cannot (pre-order emit forces an eager strip). `scanTokens`/`resolveImportPath` already filter MISSING and DIRECTORY imports (they stat via isRegularFile), so those keep their marker verbatim today — the ONLY unhandled class is exists-but-unreadable. `fs` is `import { promises as fs } from "node:fs"` (file-injector.ts:4); VERIFIED `fs.constants.R_OK === 4` is reachable through the promises namespace at runtime (Node ≥18) — NO new import is required.
2. INPUT: `injectMarkdown` (file-injector.ts:824-877). The Step 3.5 loop at lines 848-856 currently reads: `const injectable: {index:number;prefixLen:number;abs:string}[] = []; for (const r of records) { try { const st = await fs.stat(r.abs); if (st.isFile()) injectable.push(r); } catch { /* leave verbatim */ } }`. `records` come from `scanTokens` (only RESOLVED, i.e. existing-regular-file imports). `injectable` feeds Step 4 (strip, 862-865), Step 5 (emit, 867-868), Step 6 (recurse, 870-875).
3. LOGIC (implement PRD §5.4/§5.6 step-4/§12.5/§12.16 verbatim-on-error for the markdown path): In the Step 3.5 loop, after the `isFile()` check, ADD `await fs.access(r.abs, fs.constants.R_OK);` so a record is `injectable` iff stat-OK && isFile && readable. Keep it inside the existing try/catch so a thrown access (ENOENT/EACCES) drops the record (→ verbatim, not stripped, not recursed). Concretely: `if (!st.isFile()) continue; await fs.access(r.abs, fs.constants.R_OK); injectable.push(r);` Update the Step 3.5 comment block to say it now gates on READABILITY (not just existence) to honor PRD §5.4/§12.5, and add a ONE-LINE residual note: an import readable at Step 3.5 that fails at Step 6's readFile (TOCTOU) or whose image resizeImage throws (not returns null) will still be stripped — accepted narrow residual, backstopped by injectFile's try/catch (no crash, no block). TDD: FIRST add a failing regression case to file-injector.test.mjs modeled EXACTLY on E4 (lines 600-615): create `api.md` + `notes.md` (notes.md body = `Notes intro.\n#@api.md\nNotes end.\n`), `fsSync.chmodSync(api, 0o000)`, guard `if (process.getuid() === 0) {console.log('      (skipped: running as root)'); return;}` (chmod is ineffective under root), call `mod.injectFiles('Read #@notes.md', [], FIX)` inside try, assert (a) `r.text.includes('#@api.md')===true` (marker verbatim in notes.md's block), (b) `!r.text.includes('<file name="'+api+'">')` (no block for api.md), (c) `r.injected===1` (only notes.md delivered), and in `finally` `fsSync.chmodSync(api, 0o644)` so TMPDIR cleanup works. Place it right after E4. Pick an unused case id consistent with the harness convention (e.g. 'E5'). Run it RED against the unmodified Step 3.5, then implement the gate, then GREEN.
4. OUTPUT: `injectMarkdown` Step 3.5 now requires readability, so an unreadable markdown import keeps its `#@`/`@` marker byte-for-byte verbatim in the parent's emitted block and appends no block for it — matching the top-level path and PRD §5.4/§10 row 'Read throws (permissions) → token left verbatim'. Missing/directory/readable-text/markdown/binary behavior is byte-for-byte unchanged (access passes for readable regular files; isFile still rejects directories). New harness case E5 locks it in.
5. MOCKING: None. The unreadable file is produced with chmod 000 (root-skipped, same idiom as E4). `injectFiles` is driven directly through the existing jiti-loaded `mod`, identical to every other case.
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the `injectMarkdown` Step 3.5 inline comment (file-injector.ts ~837-847) to document that the gate now confirms READABILITY (fs.access R_OK), not just existence, citing PRD §5.4/§12.5, and noting the narrow TOCTOU + resizeImage-throw residual. The README 'What gets injected' table (README.md:52) and markdown-imports section already state 'permission error → Left as written' — VERIFY they still match the now-correct code (no edit expected; this fix brings the code into compliance with existing docs). Do not create a separate docs subtask.

subtasks.title: Un-claim abs from injectedSet on injectFile failure (claim ⟺ delivered)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/bugfix/001_0aea8e8cbcc6/architecture/code_changes_analysis.md (§'Fix site 2') and the independent review's safety analysis. `injectFile` (file-injector.ts:676-728) claims `abs` at line 684 (`state.injectedSet.add(abs)`) AFTER stat+isFile but BEFORE `fs.readFile` (line 689). If readFile/resizeImage throws, the catch (lines 726-727) returns false but LEAVES abs claimed — the path is 'claimed but not delivered', so any later reference to the same abs is suppressed (injectMarkdown Step 6 `if (state.injectedSet.has(r.abs)) continue` at ~874; processTokenStream ~658) without retry. The PRD §12 reference pseudocode comments 'Claims abs on success' — the code contradicts this. SAFETY (independently verified): cycle/termination (§12.13, §5.6 step 2) keys on claims for SUCCESSFULLY-READ markdown (a failed read never reaches injectMarkdown, so it produces no scan and no cycle through a failed node can exist); deleting a failed claim cannot reopen a cycle, and injectMarkdown Step 2's idempotent self-claim refers to the success path. After S1's R_OK gate, an unreadable import is never injectable/never recursed/never claimed, so S2's value is the TOCTOU window and the image-resizeImage-THROWS residual plus restoring the clean invariant; it does NOT fix the strip alone (S1 does). This subtask depends on S1 so the pair is coherent.
2. INPUT: `injectFile` (file-injector.ts:676-728): claim at line 684, the try-block body 686-725, and the catch at 726-727 (`} catch { return false; // read/resize error → leave THIS token verbatim (PRD §5.4, §12.5) }`). `state.injectedSet` is a `Set<string>` (State interface, file-injector.ts:320). `injectFile` IS exported (line 676), so it is directly callable from the harness with a hand-built `state`.
3. LOGIC: In the `injectFile` catch block, BEFORE `return false`, add `state.injectedSet.delete(abs);` so the failure path revokes the pre-read claim (claim ⟺ delivered). Add/adjust the catch comment to note the un-claim (failed read/resize must not poison the dedup set). TDD: FIRST write a failing unit test using the EXPORTED `injectFile` directly (jiti erases the unexported `State` type, so a structurally-compatible plain object works). Build a minimal state: `const state = { injectedSet: new Set(), blocks: [], images: [], remaining: null, count: 0, paged: 0, bareAt: false };` create an unreadable regular file (chmod 000, root-skip guard like E4), call `await mod.injectFile(unreadablePath, state, { cwd: TMPDIR, getContextUsage: () => undefined })`, then `assert(state.injectedSet.has(unreadablePath) === false, 'failed injectFile must NOT leave the path claimed (poisoned dedup)')` and `assert(result === false, 'failed read returns false')`. Run RED (currently the path STAYS claimed), implement the delete, run GREEN. Keep it as one focused harness case (reuse the chmod-000/finally-restore idiom).
4. OUTPUT: `injectFile`'s failure path un-claims `abs`. `injectedSet` now means 'delivered', not 'attempted', so a later duplicate reference to a path that failed delivery can retry (and fail/verbatim harmlessly) instead of being silently suppressed. No success-path behavior changes (the delete only fires in the catch).
5. MOCKING: None. The unreadable regular file is chmod 000 (root-skipped). `injectFile` is called directly with a hand-built state — no ctx services are exercised on the failure path (stat OK → claim → readFile throws → catch → delete → return false before any block/image push).
6. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the `injectFile` JSDoc/inline comment above the claim (file-injector.ts ~678-684) and the catch comment (~727) to state the claim is now revoked on failure ('claim ⟺ delivered'), citing PRD §12.5. No user-facing/config/API surface change — this is internal dedup hygiene; the README is unaffected.

milestones.title: Issue 2: Confirm the already-applied T2.S1-f test-isolation fix + sync changeset docs
milestones.description: Issue 2 is a test-harness robustness gap, NOT a product bug. REALITY-CHECK (verified by git diff and a full suite run, and independently confirmed): the isolation fix for `T2.S1-f` is ALREADY PRESENT in the working tree (uncommitted) — the whole T2.S1-* family was rewritten to capture a GLOBAL_BASELINE and assert relative to it; T2.S1-f neutralizes the project dedicated file (`PROJ_FILE_PATH='{}'`) and uses `flip` (negation of baseline). The suite reports 120 passed / 0 failed in a dogfooded-global environment. So this milestone is VERIFY-and-retain, plus the final changeset-level documentation sweep (Mode B) that must run after all implementing subtasks.

tasks.title: Verify & retain the T2.S1-f readConfig test-isolation fix
tasks.description: Confirm that the PRD Issue 2 'Suggested Fix' is already realized in file-injector.test.mjs (uncommitted), that it correctly isolates the project dedicated file so T2.S1-f passes regardless of the developer's real global config, and that the full suite is green. Do NOT re-implement from scratch — the fix is in the tree; only re-apply the PRD pattern if verification finds it absent (e.g. a fresh checkout). The implementation behavior under test (dedicated file overrides settings.json key within a scope, gated by trust) is intentional and correct and is NOT changed.

subtasks.title: Verify T2.S1-f isolation fix is present, correct vs PRD §4.6, and the suite is green
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/bugfix/001_0aea8e8cbcc6/architecture/test_harness_analysis.md (§'Issue 2 — the fix is ALREADY in the working tree'). `git status` shows `file-injector.test.mjs` MODIFIED (uncommitted) and `file-injector.ts` UNMODIFIED. `git diff file-injector.test.mjs` shows the T2.S1-a/c/d/f family rewritten around a module-level `GLOBAL_BASELINE = await mod.readConfig({cwd: <nonexistent>, isProjectTrusted:()=>false})`. The current T2.S1-f (file-injector.test.mjs ~1999-2018): captures `fileValid` (existing PROJ_FILE_PATH content), writes `{}` to PROJ_FILE_PATH to NEUTRALIZE the higher-precedence dedicated file, writes `settings.json` `fileInjector.markdownBareAtImports = flip` (flip = negation of baseline), asserts untrusted→baseline and trusted→flip, restores in finally. A full run prints `Result: 120 passed, 0 failed` with `✓ case T2.S1-f`. This EXACTLY matches the PRD Issue 2 'Suggested Fix'.
2. INPUT: file-injector.test.mjs T2.S1-f (~lines 1999-2018) and its helpers `PROJ_FILE_PATH` (~1976), `PROJ_SETTINGS_PATH` (~1975), `writeSettings` (~1977), `GLOBAL_BASELINE` (~1908). PRD §4.6 config sources / trust gate (the PRD.md main doc, referenced within h3.1).
3. LOGIC: VERIFY (read-only checks) that (a) `git diff file-injector.test.mjs` contains the T2.S1-f isolation (PROJ_FILE_PATH='{}' neutralization + GLOBAL_BASELINE + flip + finally restore); (b) the case asserts BOTH untrusted→baseline AND trusted→flip AND that they differ (the trust gate); (c) the dedicated-file precedence case T2.S1-g still proves the dedicated file overrides the settings.json key (so neutralizing it in T2.S1-f is the right isolation, not a masking of real behavior). Then RUN `node ./file-injector.test.mjs` and confirm `✓ case T2.S1-f` and `Result: 121 passed, 0 failed` (120 + the new E5 from P1.M1.T1.S1). If verification finds the isolation ABSENT (a fresh checkout without the uncommitted edit), apply the exact PRD Issue 2 'Suggested Fix' pattern: capture PROJ_FILE_PATH, write '{}' to it for the case duration, use flip, restore in finally — mirroring sibling T2.S1-e. Ensure the change is RETAINED (do not revert it). The product code (readConfig) is NOT modified.
4. OUTPUT: A confirmed, suite-green T2.S1-f that is deterministic regardless of the developer's real global ~/.pi/agent config. No source change to file-injector.ts.
5. MOCKING: None. readConfig reads the REAL global sources at runtime; the test isolates the PROJECT sources under TMPDIR and asserts relative to the captured baseline, which is the correct hermetic pattern.
6. DOCS: none — test-harness-only change with no user-facing/config/API surface. (The optional bare-@ behavior it configures is already documented in README.md:103 and PRD §4.6.)

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final Mode-B documentation sweep that runs AFTER every implementing subtask in this changeset. This is a correctness bugfix that brings the CODE into compliance with EXISTING documentation (README already states 'permission error → Left as written'; the code was the divergent party). The sweep therefore primarily VERIFIES consistency and decides whether any overview touch or residual note is warranted. It must not be skipped even though the expected doc delta is small.

subtasks.title: Sweep README.md + overview docs for consistency with the verbatim-on-error fix
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_b7c78dbf73b1/bugfix/001_0aea8e8cbcc6/architecture/system_context.md and test_harness_analysis.md. The README 'What gets injected' table (README.md:52) already reads 'Missing file, directory, or permission error | Left as written. Nothing is appended.' and the markdown-imports section (README.md:82-84, 103, 111) describes relative-only/code-exempt/bare-@ semantics. PRD §10 (PRD.md:889) row 'Read throws (permissions) | Caught; token left verbatim' and §5.4/§12.5/§12.16 likewise already specify the now-correct behavior. So this bugfix does NOT introduce new user-facing behavior or config — it makes the code match existing docs. The image-resizeImage-THROWS residual is an obscure internal edge (resizeImage is contractually null-on-failure) and is documented in code comments by P1.M1.T1.S1.
2. INPUT: The completed P1.M1.T1.S1 (R_OK gate + code comment + E5 test), P1.M1.T1.S2 (un-claim + comment), P1.M2.T1.S1 (verified T2.S1-f). README.md (whole file, ~8.5KB) and the changeset's code-comment updates.
3. LOGIC: Read README.md in full. For each doc that touches the injection error/permission surface, confirm it still describes the fixed behavior accurately: (a) README.md:52 table — 'permission error → Left as written' now holds for markdown TRANSITIVE imports too (it already said so generally; confirm no qualifying footnote is needed); (b) README.md:82-84/103 markdown-imports section — confirm nothing implies a markdown import of an unreadable file would be stripped; (c) decide whether to surface the resizeImage-throw residual to users — RECOMMEND NOT (it is an internal detail, resizeImage returns null not throws, and surfacing it would confuse the happy-path reader); if you judge a one-line note adds value, add it ONLY to the code comment (already done in S1), not the README. If everything is already consistent (expected), make NO edit and record that decision. If any README sentence is now inaccurate or could be tightened to mention transitive-import parity, make the minimal edit.
4. OUTPUT: README.md and any overview doc are consistent with the fixed code. Either a recorded 'no change needed — docs already correct' decision, or a minimal accuracy edit. The PRD.md is READ-ONLY and must NOT be modified.
5. MOCKING: None — documentation review only.
6. DOCS: This IS the documentation task (Mode B). It declares dependencies on ALL implementing subtasks so it runs last and sees the final code+comment state.
